claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**First question: How old is your child?**

Based on their age:
- **Under 16**: They may automatically qualify
- **16 to 19**: They may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me:
1. Your child's name
2. Their age (or date of birth)

If you have multiple children, we can check eligibility for each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me check the eligibility for Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Since Alex is 9 years old (under 16), that's a good start. Now I need to check a few more requirements.

**Next question: Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

At 9 years old, I would expect Alex is not working, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all. He's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that are claimed by Alex directly (not by you as the parent).

For a 9-year-old in primary school, this is typically not the case, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. I'm the one who handles all of that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex in any kind of apprenticeship program?**

Again, for a 9-year-old in primary school, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm. Certain types of apprenticeships can affect eligibility for older children.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's only 9 and still in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Question: Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **ANY** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do any of these apply to you?** The most common one is simply that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That satisfies the residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are you responsible for Alex?**

You're considered responsible for Alex if **ANY** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep (paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care - this includes food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

**Do any of these apply to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another guardian or family member
- Anyone else who might be claiming

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time. 

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: What is Alex's current living situation?**

I need to check if any special circumstances apply:

- **Is Alex currently in hospital?**
- **Is Alex in local authority care?**
- **Is Alex in a fostering arrangement?**
- **Or is Alex living normally at home with you?**

Which of these describes Alex's current situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question: Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

This would mean the council is providing financial support specifically for Alex's housing or upkeep costs.

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I'm supporting him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Excellent News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on the information you've provided, **you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Why You're Eligible:

✅ Alex is 9 years old (under 16)  
✅ Alex is not working  
✅ Alex doesn't receive benefits in his own right  
✅ Alex is not in an apprenticeship  
✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)  
✅ You are responsible for Alex (living with him)  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ Alex lives at home with you  
✅ The local council is not paying for Alex's care  

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate

### Important Information:

- **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay a tax charge. Between £60,000-£80,000, the charge gradually increases until it equals the Child Benefit amount

**You can apply here:** https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16 and qualifies automatically for age requirements; (2) Alex is not working (in primary school); (3) Alex does not receive any benefits in his own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for him; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home, not in hospital, care, or fostering; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria have been met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 9-year-old child Alex. All eligibility requirements have been satisfied: the child is under 16, living at home with the claimant who is responsible for him, the claimant lives in the UK, no one else is claiming for the child, and no disqualifying factors apply.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

All eligibility criteria have been met.
